notes: Instructor discretion (-1) for assessment/teaching; explicit privacy warnings (T3); disclosure recommended (T5); pedagogical reflection/redesign urged (T8).

Text is a meta-description of a past event and a resource portal. It mentions 'ChatGPT' and 'online classes' but contains no policy directives, permissions, restrictions, or substantive guidance on GenAI use.
Introductory text framing AI as a standard tool; implies permission/encouragement for general use. No specific restrictions or redesigns mentioned in this chunk.

Text lists principles (equity, security, integrity, redesign) and policy development steps. No specific use-case permissions or bans are stated, so sentiment is neutral for assessment/research. Teaching/administration sentiment is positive due to encouragement of integration and structured policy adoption.
Text describes specific pedagogical reforms (T8) and integration of AI tutors/assessment (T1). Strongly positive tone toward teaching/assessment/research integration.

Text describes technical limitations (hallucinations, black box) and advises caution/verification. No specific policy rules, permissions, or bans are stated, but the tone is cautionary across all use cases.

T2 coded due to explicit mention of AI-generated video. T8 coded due to call to 'imagine new ways of teaching'. Sentiment for research is +2 (strongly encouraged/celebrated). Teaching is +1 (permissive/encouraging redesign). No specific policy rules for assessment or administration.

Privacy cited as reason for banning commercial tools (T3). Institutional tool mandated (T7). Fraud penalties for unauthorized use (T4). Supportive use permitted (T1), but assessment submission is strictly restricted (-2).

Strong integrity focus (misconduct, no detection tools). T3/T7 via Microsoft Copilot privacy specifics. T5 via 'proper acknowledgement' requirement. Assessment sentiment -2 due to ban on unauthorized use.

Mentions 'programme or assessment modifications' (T8) and 'AI literacy' (T1). Privacy (T3) and integrity (T4) are framed as risks/principles rather than specific bans or disclosure rules. Sentiment is cautious (-1) for assessment/admin due to risk warnings, permissive (1) for teaching due to 'supporting adoption'.
Explicitly bans using GenAI as a substitute for assessed work, defining it as academic misconduct. Mentions code generation but does not permit it; focuses on risks and integrity violations.

Mentions 'creative visuals' (T2). States AI supports 'inclusivity and accessibility' (T6). Explicitly links uncited use to 'plagiarism and academic misconduct' (T4). Requires citation (T5). Permits use with conditions (T1, sentiment +1).

This chunk is a bibliography/reference list. It contains citations of external literature but does not contain substantive policy statements, rules, or guidelines from the institution itself. Per coding rules, meta-information or references alone do not trigger theme codes.
Strong cautionary tone regarding academic writing tools; warns of plagiarism and data privacy risks. No explicit permission granted.
Text frames GenAI as a complementary research assistant but warns against over-reliance and substitution of human agency, implying caution for research and learning.

Text is a literature review/intro. T1 via 'incorporation'; T3 via 'privacy risks'; T6 via 'unequal access'; T8 via 'curricular reform'. Sentiment +1 for teaching as it frames GenAI as complementary and lists benefits, despite noting risks.

Text is a press release about forming a committee to create rules, not the rules themselves. Mentions multimodal tools (images/code) and integrity concerns (unverified work), but no specific policy directives yet. Sentiment is cautious/permissive pending rules.

Text is a resource list. T4 coded due to explicit rejection of AI detection tools (integrity mechanism). T6 coded due to cited bias concerns. Sentiment for assessment is -1 because the institution opts out of detection, implying a non-punitive/cautionary stance on automated integrity checks. Teaching is +1 as it encourages dialogue and creative pedagogy.
Focuses on research validity, copyright, and citation. Explicitly discourages reliance on AI for sourcing (cautionary for research). Requires documentation/citation of AI use in research (T5).

Focuses on infrastructure investment (GPUs, cloud) and research support. T7 coded due to 'cloud access... through technology providers' and infrastructure procurement. Sentiment +2 for research due to 'breakthrough research' and strong investment commitment. No specific teaching/assessment policies.

Text consists entirely of speaker biographies for an event. While one speaker's role involves multimodal AI policy, the text contains no university policy statements, guidelines, or directives regarding GenAI use at Yale.

Exams are explicitly prohibited (-2). Submitted work is governed by plagiarism rules (T4), implying permissibility with attribution, but no explicit disclosure mechanism (T5) is mandated in this text.

T4 coded due to explicit warning against cheating/substitution and mention of academic consequences. T2 coded due to listing image/video/code tools. T1 coded due to permission to use for learning/research.

Vendor governance (T7) is clear due to enterprise agreements with Microsoft, Google, and OpenAI. Integration (T1) is present via pilots and proposal calls for instruction/research/admin. Sentiment is positive (+1) as the university actively enables and encourages use across domains.

Text describes communication, awareness, and monitoring strategies (websites, workshops, analytics) rather than substantive policy rules on use, integrity, or privacy. No specific use-case permissions or restrictions are stated.

Text is a descriptive tool review/glossary. T2 triggered by Adobe Firefly (images/audio/video). Sentiment +1 for research due to positive framing of ChatPDF/Perplexity for analysis. No policy directives present.
